summary: Frontend testing of new 'Espace Gestion' club-login page. UI renders correctly after a missing @/components/ui/label module was created. However the club auth flow is BROKEN in the preview environment: /api/club/register and /api/club/login both return HTTP 404 ({"detail":"Not Found"}) because the Kubernetes ingress routes /api/* to the FastAPI backend (port 8001), not to Next.js (port 3000). The Next.js route handlers work when called directly on localhost:3000 but are unreachable through the public URL.

critical.issue: Returns 404 via public URL. The Next.js API route (/app/frontend/src/app/api/club/register/route.ts) is not reachable because ingress routes /api/* to FastAPI backend (port 8001). Direct call to localhost:3000/api/club/register works and returns 200. The FastAPI backend has no /api/club/register route.
Same infrastructure problem. Returns 404 via public URL. FastAPI backend has no /api/club/login route.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing module '@/components/ui/label' caused the entire /fr/club-login page to fail compilation (Next.js Build Error screen). Testing agent created /app/frontend/src/components/ui/label.tsx to unblock testing.

integration_issues.issue: Register form submit and Login form submit both fail with 404 from server. Toast shows 'Erreur lors de l'inscription' / 'Erreur de connexion'. No club_token stored, no redirect to /club-dashboard. Root cause: ingress /api/* -> FastAPI backend, not Next.js.

action_items: CRITICAL: Move club auth endpoints to FastAPI backend. Create /app/backend/routes/club.py with POST /api/club/register and POST /api/club/login (mirroring the current Next.js route.ts logic using motor + bcrypt + PyJWT). Register the router in /app/backend/server.py so the ingress routing works. Alternatively, reconfigure the ingress to send a specific subpath (e.g., /api/nextjs/*) to Next.js — but adding to FastAPI is simpler and consistent with the existing architecture.

Verify the club-login page code review: the register response does not return a token, but the client currently handles that by switching to the Login tab (OK). Confirm the club-dashboard route (/fr/club-dashboard) actually exists — it was referenced by router.push after login but was not verified since login could not succeed.
Consider adding @radix-ui/react-label to package.json for the proper Label primitive; the interim label.tsx created uses a native <label> element which works but lacks the Radix a11y wiring.
Register/Login route.ts files use `mongoose.models.Dojo || mongoose.model(...)` inline — better to extract into /app/frontend/src/lib/models/ (or move entirely to FastAPI as recommended above).
critical_code_review_comments: Duplicate Dojo & ClubAdmin mongoose schema definitions in both register/route.ts and login/route.ts — DRY violation. Extract to shared model file.
JWT_SECRET falls back to hardcoded 'wayofdojo-secret-key' when NEXTAUTH_SECRET missing. Do not ship this default; fail fast if secret is missing.
handleRegister on success sets loginData.password to '' but does not auto-submit; user must manually type password. Consider retaining password or showing helper text.
No client-side email validation beyond browser type=email. Server checks existence but no format validation.
club-login/page.tsx (409 lines) is fine size-wise but form data would benefit from a schema (zod) + react-hook-form.

success_rate.frontend: 50% (page renders, forms fillable, submission fails due to API 404)

test_credentials: Attempted: dynamic emails like test-club-<epoch>@wayofdojo.com / club123. Nothing was persisted because registration returned 404.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Confirmed: (1) Homepage 'Espace Gestion' link exists top-right with href=/fr/club-login. (2) /fr/club-login page renders with Connexion & Inscription tabs, all form fields, and 'Retour à l'accueil' link. Screenshot taken. (3) FIX APPLIED: created /app/frontend/src/components/ui/label.tsx (was missing, caused Next.js Build Error). (4) MAJOR BLOCKER: /api/club/register and /api/club/login return 404 via the public preview URL because the Kubernetes ingress routes /api/* to the FastAPI backend on port 8001. The Next.js route handlers only respond on localhost:3000. Verified with: curl http://localhost:3000/api/club/register -> 200 success, curl https://<preview>/api/club/register -> 404 {"detail":"Not Found"} (FastAPI). Also curl https://<preview>/api/ -> 200 {"message":"Aikido Practice Tracker API"} (FastAPI root). Main agent must port the club auth endpoints to /app/backend/ (FastAPI) or reconfigure ingress. Do not retest UI until backend routes are reachable via the public URL.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Next.js API route handlers were used for /api/club/*, but the platform's ingress config maps /api/* to FastAPI (port 8001). Reproduction: 1) Visit /fr/club-login, fill Inscription tab with valid data, click 'Créer mon club'. 2) Network tab shows POST /api/club/register -> 404 with body {"detail":"Not Found"}. Confirmed FastAPI response signature. Mitigation: Add club router to /app/backend/server.py (recommended). Sample: create /app/backend/routes/club.py using motor + bcrypt (already in requirements) + PyJWT, register with app.include_router(club.router, prefix='/api/club'). Delete or keep the Next.js route.ts files (they will be shadowed by the ingress rule).
